behaviors.description: Renamed files appear only in `renamed` and are not double-counted as `added` + `deleted`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS and MOCK-HIDE are both high: rename detection is a Git behavior (similarity thresholds/status format) that in-process mocks can easily oversimplify. REAL-INFRA applies because the CLI is exercised as a subprocess against a real repo/worktree. No LLM or multi-step agent dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit `a.txt`, then rename it to `b.txt` with content preserved enough for rename detection. Run the CLI via subprocess and parse stdout report. Assert `renamed == 1`, `added == 0`, `deleted == 0` for that change set.

behaviors.description: Binary diffs do not crash the tool, and binary files are counted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-HIDE is high: mocked diff text often misses real binary diff output/encoding edge cases. REAL-INFRA applies because this is about real git diff behavior on binary content and CLI subprocess execution. No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify it, and run the CLI subprocess over recent changes. Assert exit code is success, process does not crash, and the report includes the binary file in the correct category/count.

behaviors.description: A repository with no commits yields an empty report, not an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA matters because unborn-HEAD behavior is a real Git repository state, often surfaced via subprocess exit codes/messages. Mocks can miss this exact failure mode. No LLM or workflow dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a fresh repo with `git init` and no commits. Run the CLI subprocess pointed at that repo. Assert successful exit and structured empty report (all categories zero/empty), with no traceback on stderr.

behaviors.description: Reported counts match the repository’s true change history.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against actual Git history semantics across multiple commit states. MOCK-MISS is high because handcrafted fixtures can drift from real history edge cases. Exercising the CLI subprocess against real commits is the true failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a scripted sequence producing known totals across added/deleted/renamed/modified files over several commits. Run the CLI subprocess for the target range and assert each reported category count exactly matches the precomputed ground truth.

behaviors.description: Output is identical for equivalent staged vs unstaged change sets.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real index/worktree semantics and how the CLI reads Git state. Mocks can hide differences in staged/unstaged plumbing commands and status interpretation. Requires real repo state transitions plus CLI subprocess execution.
behaviors.test_description: Create identical content changes in two temp repos (or reset between runs): case A staged (`git add`), case B unstaged only. Run the CLI subprocess in each case with the same analysis scope and compare normalized structured stdout; assert exact equality.
